unit.name: Autoregressive generation, architectures, and teacher forcing
unit.description: Reviews left-to-right autoregressive language modeling, architecture choices, and the standard maximum-likelihood training setup.
unit.summary: This segment reviews autoregressive language modeling: at each step the model scores the vocabulary, applies softmax to obtain the next-token distribution, chooses a token, and feeds that prediction back in recursively until the sequence ends [ts=528s]. It then connects task type to architecture, describing encoder-decoder systems as the usual choice for less open-ended tasks like machine translation and decoder-only autoregressive models as the common choice for more open-ended generation, while emphasizing that this is a practical convention rather than a hard constraint [ts=577s] [ts=638s]. The lecture revisits maximum-likelihood training as next-token prediction under teacher forcing, where gold previous tokens are provided during training [ts=672s]. It distinguishes this from generation-time student forcing, where the model must condition on its own earlier outputs, and clarifies that “autoregressive” refers to generating left-to-right by chaining over previously generated tokens [ts=729s] [ts=742s].

key_points.text: Autoregressive generation predicts one token at a time from a softmax next-token distribution and feeds predictions back into the model.
Encoder-decoder models are commonly preferred for less open-ended tasks such as machine translation, while decoder-only models are common for more open-ended generation.
Teacher forcing trains by maximizing the probability of the next token given gold previous tokens.
Student forcing uses the model's own generated text as context at generation time.
Autoregressive means generating left to right by conditioning on tokens just generated via the chain rule.

unit_summary_for_generation: This segment reviews autoregressive language modeling: at each step the model scores the vocabulary, applies softmax to obtain the next-token distribution, chooses a token, and feeds that prediction back in recursively until the sequence ends [ts=528s]. It then connects task type to architecture, describing encoder-decoder systems as the usual choice for less open-ended tasks like machine translation and decoder-only autoregressive models as the common choice for more open-ended generation, while emphasizing that this is a practical convention rather than a hard constraint [ts=577s] [ts=638s]. The lecture revisits maximum-likelihood training as next-token prediction under teacher forcing, where gold previous tokens are provided during training [ts=672s]. It distinguishes this from generation-time student forcing, where the model must condition on its own earlier outputs, and clarifies that “autoregressive” refers to generating left-to-right by chaining over previously generated tokens [ts=729s] [ts=742s].

kp_catalog.name: Autoregressive language modeling
kp_catalog.description: Training a model to predict the next token from its prefix, used directly for decoder pretraining and as a baseline pretraining objective more broadly. Autoregressive models make sequence modeling tractable by factoring p(x) into conditional next-part distributions over an ordered decomposition of x.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because autoregressive language modeling formalizes how training a model to predict the next token from its prefix, used directly for decoder pretraining and as a baseline pretraining objective more broadly, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.

kp_catalog.name: Teacher forcing and student forcing
kp_catalog.description: The contrast between training with gold previous tokens and generating with the model's own previous outputs.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because teacher forcing and student forcing clarifies the contrast between training with gold previous tokens and generating with the model's own previous outputs, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Open-ended generation and evaluation
kp_catalog.description: A way to categorize generation tasks by how many valid outputs they permit, with entropy used as a formal intuition for openness. Open-ended tasks have many valid answers and graded quality, so evaluation must move beyond exact labels.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because open-ended generation and evaluation formalizes how a way to categorize generation tasks by how many valid outputs they permit, with entropy used as a formal intuition for openness, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.
